---
# Chaindoc Labs — E-Signature Laws (Full Catalog)

> Open dataset and narrative guides on electronic-signature law and contract drafting by jurisdiction. Statute-grounded references on eIDAS (EU), ESIGN/UETA (US), the UK assimilated regime, and country-by-country handbooks.

> Comprehensive catalog of every page on the site. For a curated overview, see {{ '/llms.txt' | absolute_url }}.
{% assign all_pages = site.pages | where_exp: "p", "p.url" | sort: "url" %}

## Foundations
{% for page in all_pages %}{% if page.url == "/" or page.url == "/glossary/" or page.url == "/awesome/" %}
- [{{ page.title }}]({{ '/md/' | append: page.path | absolute_url }}){% if page.description %}: {{ page.description | strip_newlines }}{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endfor %}

## Jurisdiction Guides
{% for page in all_pages %}{% assign head = page.url | slice: 0, 6 %}{% if head == "/docs/" and page.url != "/docs/" %}
- [{{ page.title }}]({{ '/md/' | append: page.path | absolute_url }}){% if page.description %}: {{ page.description | strip_newlines }}{% elsif page.statute %}: {{ page.statute }} ({{ page.jurisdiction }}).{% endif %}{% endif %}{% endfor %}

## Machine-Readable Data

- [Laws Dataset (JSON)]({{ '/data/laws.json' | absolute_url }}): Structured row per jurisdiction matching the narrative guides above.
- [Laws Dataset Schema]({{ '/data/schema.json' | absolute_url }}): JSON Schema (Draft-07) for the laws dataset.
- [Handbook Schema]({{ '/data/handbook/schema.json' | absolute_url }}): JSON Schema for a single contract-drafting record.
- [Handbook Bundle — DE]({{ '/data/handbook/de.json' | absolute_url }}): All German handbook records as a single array.
- [Handbook Bundle — US]({{ '/data/handbook/us.json' | absolute_url }}): All US handbook records.
- [Handbook Bundle — UK]({{ '/data/handbook/uk.json' | absolute_url }}): All UK handbook records.
- [Handbook Bundle — FR]({{ '/data/handbook/fr.json' | absolute_url }}): All French handbook records.

## Source Policy

- Primary sources only: statutes, regulations, official court rulings. No Wikipedia, vendor whitepapers, or law-firm marketing.
- ≥2 independent primary sources per fact, each timestamped with an `accessed` date.
- `confidence: low` flags single-source facts.
- Disclaimer: every guide ends with an explicit "informational, not legal advice. Last verified: …" block.

## License

- Content (guides, handbook prose): CC-BY 4.0.
- Code, schemas, CI scripts: MIT.
